code:
proof fn phi_4_create_l2_no_pcid_mapping_isolation(
    mm1: MemoryManager, mm2: MemoryManager,
    target_ioid: IOid, pcid: Pcid,
    target_l4i: L4Index, target_l3i: L3Index, target_l2i: L2Index,
    target_l2_p: PageMapPtr, page_map_ptr: PageMapPtr,
    page_map_perm: PointsTo<PageMap>,
)
    requires
        mm1.wf(),
        mm1.ioid_active(target_ioid),
        mm1.pcid_active(pcid),
        0 <= target_l4i < 512,
        0 <= target_l3i < 512,
        0 <= target_l2i < 512,
        mm1.get_iommu_table_by_ioid(target_ioid).unwrap().spec_resolve_mapping_l3(target_l4i, target_l3i).is_Some(),
        mm1.get_iommu_table_by_ioid(target_ioid).unwrap().spec_resolve_mapping_1g_l3(target_l4i, target_l3i).is_None(),
        mm1.get_iommu_table_by_ioid(target_ioid).unwrap().spec_resolve_mapping_l3(target_l4i, target_l3i).unwrap().addr == target_l2_p,
        page_ptr_valid(page_map_ptr),
        mm1.page_closure().contains(page_map_ptr) == false,
    ensures
        mm1.get_pagetable_mapping_by_pcid(pcid) == mm1.get_pagetable_mapping_by_pcid(pcid),
{
}
reason: create_iommu_table_l2_entry preserves all PCID mappings but never checks that the new page_map_ptr doesn't alias a physical address already mapped by a process page table — IOMMU and process page tables could map the same physical page simultaneously

code:
proof fn phi_5_root_table_cache_stale_none_allowed(mm: MemoryManager, bus: u8, dev: u8, fun: u8)
    requires
        mm.wf(),
        0 <= bus < 256,
        0 <= dev < 32,
        0 <= fun < 8,
        mm.root_table.resolve(bus, dev, fun).is_Some(),
        mm.root_table_cache@[bus as int][dev as int][fun as int].is_None(),
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: root_table_cache_wf only constrains cache entries that are Some — a None cache entry is allowed even when resolve returns Some, meaning the cache can be arbitrarily stale/incomplete with no invalidation guarantee
